# OCA Watersports

> OCA Watersports is a jet ski and pontoon boat rental company located at 12817 Harbor Rd, Ocean City, Maryland 21842. Founded in 2010 (formerly Under The Bridge Watersports), OCA operates the Famous 6 Mile Ride — a 6-mile southbound jet ski route from the Ocean City inlet to the Verrazano Bridge along Assateague Island National Seashore. Phone: 410-629-RIDE (7433). Email: ride@ocawatersports.com. Rating: 4.4 stars, 640+ Google reviews.

## Services

- [Jet Ski Rentals](https://www.ocawatersports.com/jet-skis.html): Sea-Doo GTX jet ski rentals, $129+tax per ski, 70-minute rides, 1-3 riders per ski, 450 lb max, minimum age 16 to operate, minimum age 21 to rent without guardian.
- [Pontoon Boat Rentals](https://www.ocawatersports.com/pontoon-boats.html): Private pontoon boat rentals for up to 12 guests. 1hr/$199, 2hr/$329, 3hr/$419 +tax. You drive — temporary boater's license issued at dock. Access to Assateague Island bay-side sandbars, Seacrets, and the entire OC bay.
- [The 6 Mile Ride](https://www.ocawatersports.com/the-6-mile-ride.html): OCA's signature jet ski experience — 6 miles south from the OC Inlet to the Verrazano Bridge along Assateague Island National Seashore. Wild horses, dolphins, sandbars, 37 miles of protected coastline.

## Key Facts

- Location: 12817 Harbor Rd, Ocean City, MD 21842 (north side of Rt. 50 drawbridge, direct bay access, next to The Angler restaurant)
- Jet ski price: $129+tax per ski, 70 minutes of ride time, Sea-Doo GTX, up to 3 riders per ski, 450 lb max
- Pontoon price: $199 (1hr), $329 (2hr), $419 (3hr) +tax, 100% private, up to 12 guests, 1,900-2,100 lb max
- Add-ons: Bluetooth speaker $50, Fishing package $39, Crabbing package $39, Tube $75
- Jet ski speed: 40-42 mph on open water
- Reviews: 4.4 stars, 640+ Google reviews
- Founded: 2010 as Under The Bridge Watersports; rebranded to OCA Watersports
- Route: 6-mile southbound only, no guided tour, riders operate independently after safety briefing
- Assateague Island: 37-mile barrier island, National Seashore protected by NPS since 1965, wild horses roaming since the 1600s
